stories.title: U.S.-Iran talks are uncertain as ceasefire is set to expire
stories.description: As the clock ticks down on the ceasefire, Iran says it may not attend fresh talks with the U.S. That's as a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon appears to be holding.

stories.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
stories.description: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
stories.description: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

stories.title: Ukraine's fast-growing defense tech sector takes center stage in Iran war
stories.description: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.

stories.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
stories.description: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

stories.title: Outrage over Israeli soldier's vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli military says it views with "great severity" the actions of the soldier, as the US ambassador demands "swift" consequences.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: Middle East war live: US, Iran warn they are ready for war as peace talks in limbo
stories.description: The United States and Iran both warned they were ready for war as peace talks that President Donald Trump had announced would resume on Tuesday in Pakistan were left in limbo. The clock is ticking on a ceasefire deal between the two countries that is set to expire overnight Tuesd...

stories.title: EU to gauge member states' willingness to take measures against Israel
stories.description: EU member states will discuss their cooperation agreement with Israel, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said Monday, though she remained cautious about the outcome of the talks. Spain is leading the call to suspend the deal as attitudes towards Israel among key EU member state...

stories.title: Israel confirms its soldier destroyed Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli military confirmed a viral photo of one of its soldiers smashing a statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon is "authentic," and said its launched an investigation into the incident. Taken in the Christian village of Debel, close to the Israeli border, i...

stories.title: 'Trump has boxed himself into a corner', expert says as fate of Iran peace talks uncertain
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Jennie Shin, Rosemary Kelanic, Director of the Middle East programme at the think tank Defense Priorities, says that US President Donald Trump "has boxed himself into a corner", explaining that "this war has failed, the war has put the United States in a...

stories.title: Iran says 'no decision' on joining new talks, with US delegation set to depart
stories.description: Iran said it had yet to decide whether to attend a new round of peace negotiations with the United States, as Washington's delegation prepared to depart for Pakistan on Monday. The uncertainty over the talks on ending the war that engulfed the region came as US President Donald T...

stories.title: What exit strategy? Trump, Iranians send mixed signals as truce deadline looms
stories.description: A container ship seized in the Gulf, a ceasefire deadline that's running out fast and plenty of bargaining table brinkmanship between the U-S and Iran. stories.title: 'Hierarchy of sovereignty': Israel claims authority over 'which Lebanese areas are inhabitable'
stories.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Nadim Houry, Executive Director of the Arab Reform Initiative (ARI). According to Houry, the “Yellow Line doctrine” is not a security measure, but rather a military tool for territorial control. Drawing on recent developments in Gaza, Syria, an...

stories.title: Iran war forces job losses, reverse migration in India’s ceramic hub
stories.description: The ceramics industry in Gujarat's Morbi has been badly hit by fuel crisis, with most units closed and workers laid off.

stories.title: US veterans arrested in Capitol during protest against the war on Iran
stories.description: US veterans were arrested while staging a protest in the US Capitol building in opposition to the war on Iran.

stories.title: US is ‘pretty far behind where they started’ the war on Iran
stories.description: Daniel Benaim says the US missed the early off-ramps to declare victory over Iran.

stories.title: Global fallout grows over Israeli soldier smashing Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: Outrage continues to grow over the Israeli soldier who was photographed desecrating a statue of Jesus Christ in Lebanon.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran spurns talks under threats; Trump says blockade stays
stories.description: Iran says will not accept negotiations 'under the shadow of threats', as Trump promises to continue blockade in Hormuz.

stories.title: Outcry grows over Israeli soldier smashing Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: Some of Donald Trump's former MAGA allies denounce the desecration of Christian religious symbol by Israeli soldier.

stories.title: EU eyes Ukraine loan, Israeli settler sanctions, after Hungarian election
stories.description: Ouster of EU 'spoiler-in-chief' Viktor Orban unlocks key foreign policy decisions in the 27-member bloc.

stories.title: How is the war on Iran affecting the Russia-Ukraine conflict?
stories.description: The global energy crisis has implications for both sides.

stories.title: ‘Biggest-ever’ US-Philippines war games begin
stories.description: MANILA: Thousands of American and Philippine troops, joined for the first time by a significant contingent of Japanese forces, began annual military exercises on Monday set against the backdrop of the Middle East war. The war games will feature live-fire exercises in the north of...

stories.title: Pope decries exploitation by world’s ‘authoritarians’
stories.description: SAURIMO: Pope Leo lamented during an event in Angola on Monday that many people in the world were being “exploited by authoritarians and defrauded by the rich”, the latest example of a forceful new speaking style he has adopted on his four-nation Africa tour. The first US pope, w...

stories.title: Hungary will carry out ICC warrant against Netanyahu, says Magyar
stories.description: BUDAPEST: Hungary’s incoming prime minister Peter Magyar said on Monday the country will execute International Criminal Court warrants against anyone, after he invited Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu — wanted under just such a warrant — to Budapest later this year. In 2024, the...

stories.title: Amnesty highlights erosion of civil liberties in Pakistan
stories.description: • Says cybercrime, anti-terror laws are weaponised to silence voices; authorities pull ads from critical newspapers• Warns world faces unprecedented rights crisis; cites use of AI, tech to expand surveillance, curb online dissent LONDON: Global rights watchdog Amnesty Inter­natio...

stories.title: India, South Korea plan $50bn trade push with new deals
stories.description: India and South Korea said on Monday that they would boost their economic ties by expanding cooperation in energy, critical minerals, shipbuilding, semiconductors and steel as they seek to double their trade to $50 billion by 2030. New Delhi and Seoul also agreed to resume and st...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: U.S. positive on Iran deal but talks still uncertain as ceasefire end nears
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards have warned of targeting any vessel attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz without permission.

stories.title: U.S. positive on Iran deal but talks still uncertain as ceasefire end nears
stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump wants an agreement that would prevent further oil price rises and stock markets shocks but ​has insisted Iran cannot have the means to develop a nuclear weapon

stories.title: How a U.S.-Iran deal over Hormuz unravelled
Iran-Israel war updates: Deep historical mistrust in Iran toward U.S. government remains, says Iran President
stories.description: Vance has not yet left for Iran talks in Pakistan, says source; Iran says 'no decision' yet on joining new round of U.S. peace talks

stories.title: Iran undecided on talks; Trump to keep blockade
stories.description: Tehran condemns U.S. attack on an Iran-flagged vessel in the Gulf of Oman, says it will make ‘necessary decisions about the future path with careful consideration of its national interests’

stories.title: Watch: Can Iran and the U.S. find middle ground?
stories.description: In the latest episode of Above the Fold, we look at the next phase of peace talks between the US and Iran appear to be uncertain ahead of Wednesday’s ceasefire deadline. stories.title: Trump says extracting enriched uranium in Iran to be lengthy process
stories.description: The US leader says the strikes carried out in June 2025 ensured "a complete and total obliteration" of Iranian nuclear sites

stories.title: Trump says US to ensure Iran has no nuclear weapons soon
stories.description: The US leader says this is going to happen "very quickly"

stories.title: Trump confident Iran to continue participating in talks
stories.description: The US leader also says "problems like they've never seen before" will arise if Iran does not participate in the dialogue

stories.title: Iran refuses to hold talks amid ongoing threats — parliament speaker
stories.description: Bagher Ghalibaf says that Tehran "is ready to demonstrate a new balance of power on the battlefield"
